MICA_OPCODE_t Union Reference
[MiCA Device API (gxio/mica.h)]

Opcode. More...


Data Fields

struct {
   uint_reg_t   size: 30
 Size of the operation source data, in bytes.
   uint_reg_t   priority: 2
 This field selects the priority level for the operation.
   uint_reg_t   engine_type: 3
 Defines which type of Engine in the MiCA should do the operation.
   uint_reg_t   src_mode: 1
 Control the usage of tde SRC_DATA User Context register.
   uint_reg_t   dest_mode: 2
 Controls the usage of the DEST_DATA User Context register.
   uint_reg_t   num_dest_bd: 5
 The number of destination Buffer Descriptors (only used when DEST_MODE is BUFF_DESC_LIST).
   uint_reg_t   extra_data_size: 6
 Number of 8-byte words of Extra Data.
   uint_reg_t   dst_size: 4
 This field specifies how much extra size is allowed for destination data, relative to source size.
   uint_reg_t   dm_specific: 11
 The use of these bits is specific to each MiCA type.
}; 
uint_reg_t word
 Word access.

Detailed Description

Opcode.

This Context User register specifies the operation to be performed.

Examples:

mica/comp/app.c.

Field Documentation

uint_reg_t MICA_OPCODE_t::size

Size of the operation source data, in bytes.

Note that the size of the destination data is a function of the size of the source data and the operation performed.

Examples:
mica/comp/app.c.

uint_reg_t MICA_OPCODE_t::priority

This field selects the priority level for the operation.

0 is highest priority, 3 is lowest.

uint_reg_t MICA_OPCODE_t::engine_type

Defines which type of Engine in the MiCA should do the operation.

Type 0 and 1 are common for all MiCAs, Type 2 through 7 are defined for each MiCA type.

Examples:
mica/comp/app.c.

uint_reg_t MICA_OPCODE_t::src_mode

Control the usage of tde SRC_DATA User Context register.

uint_reg_t MICA_OPCODE_t::dest_mode

Controls the usage of the DEST_DATA User Context register.

uint_reg_t MICA_OPCODE_t::num_dest_bd

The number of destination Buffer Descriptors (only used when DEST_MODE is BUFF_DESC_LIST).

The value is the number of descriptors, except a value 0 means 32 descriptors.

uint_reg_t MICA_OPCODE_t::extra_data_size

Number of 8-byte words of Extra Data.

The usage of Extra Data is specific to each MiCA type. A value of 0 means no Extra Data. If the amount of Extra Data is not an integral number of 8 bytes, the unused bytes at the end must be set to 0.

uint_reg_t MICA_OPCODE_t::dst_size

This field specifies how much extra size is allowed for destination data, relative to source size.

For compression/decompression the value is a multiplier of source size with value 0 being 1x, and values 1 through 9 being 1/4, 1/2, 2, 4, 8, 16, 64, 256, and 2048. For crypto the value indicates number of bytes added to source size, with values 0 through 11 being 0, 1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64, 128, 256, 512, and 1024.

Examples:
mica/comp/app.c.

uint_reg_t MICA_OPCODE_t::dm_specific

The use of these bits is specific to each MiCA type.

uint_reg_t MICA_OPCODE_t::word

Word access.
